No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion to strike all or parts of the affirmative defenses in the first amended answer is denied in its entirety. The amended answer sufficiently alleges all eight affirmative defenses. If plaintiffs need more information about those defenses, they may propound appropriate discovery. If they believe the affirmative defenses do not apply or lack an evidentiary basis, they may move for summary adjudication, file a motion in limine and/or raise the issue with the trial judge during a jury instructions conference. At this early stage of the case, in keeping with litigation norms in the Bay Area, defendants are not required to do more to allege their affirmative defenses.

Order signed.
